Functions for handling BLE characteristics and commands in the AMS (Apple Media Service) context.

# Purpose
The code defines a set of functions related to the management and operation of a Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) client, specifically for handling Apple Media Service (AMS) interactions. The functions provide a narrow scope of functionality focused on BLE characteristics and subscriptions. The file includes functions for creating and destroying AMS instances ([`ams_create`](<#ams_create>) and [`ams_destroy`](<#ams_destroy>)), handling service discovery ([`ams_handle_service_discovered`](<#ams_handle_service_discovered>)), managing characteristic subscriptions ([`ams_handle_subscribe`](<#ams_handle_subscribe>)), and processing write responses and notifications ([`ams_handle_write_response`](<#ams_handle_write_response>) and [`ams_handle_read_or_notification`](<#ams_handle_read_or_notification>)). Additionally, it includes utility functions such as [`ams_can_handle_characteristic`](<#ams_can_handle_characteristic>) to determine if a characteristic can be handled, [`ams_invalidate_all_references`](<#ams_invalidate_all_references>) to clear references, and [`ams_handle_service_removed`](<#ams_handle_service_removed>) to manage the removal of services.

The code is intended to be part of a larger system, likely a library or module, that interacts with BLE devices. It does not define a main executable but provides functions that can be used by other parts of a program to manage BLE AMS interactions. The functions do not currently implement any logic, as they contain empty bodies or return fixed values, indicating that this file may serve as a template or placeholder for future development. The inclusion of the header file `ams.h` suggests that these functions are part of a broader API for BLE communication.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ams/ams.h`
